# @hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget

A React chatbot widget with a built-in RAG pipeline. Bring your own embedding provider, LLM provider, and Supabase database with your content. The library wires the pipeline together and renders the chat UI.

## What This Is

The runtime flow is:

1. The user asks a question in the widget.
2. The library sends the text to your `EmbeddingAdapter`.
3. The resulting vector is used to query Supabase via `match_documents`.
4. The top matching chunks are assembled into a system prompt and conversation context.
5. The library sends that context to your `LLMAdapter`.
6. The answer, sources, and handoff signal are returned to the UI.

The library owns the pipeline and the UI. You own the AI providers and your content.

## What the library handles vs what you provide

| You provide | The library provides |
| --- | --- |
| Supabase project + API keys | Chat UI (toggle, messages, quick actions, lead form) |
| Embedding + LLM API keys | RAG pipeline (embed â†’ search â†’ prompt â†’ answer) |
| Your content as text chunks | Adapter factories (Gemini, OpenAI, Cohere, DeepSeek, etc.) |
| One-time SQL in Supabase | `SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL` (table, index, search function, permissions) |
| A small seed script | `ingestDocument()` helper |
| One React component to mount the widget | Source citations, loading states, handoff detection |

You do **not** need to build vector search, prompt assembly, or the chat interface from scratch.

## Prerequisites

- A React app (v17+)
- A [Supabase](https://supabase.com) project with `pgvector` enabled (v0.7.0+ recommended for 3072-dim indexes)
- An embedding API key (Gemini recommended)
- An LLM API key (Gemini, OpenAI, DeepSeek, etc.)

**Default Gemini embedding model:** `gemini-embedding-001` (3072 dimensions). Google deprecated `text-embedding-004`.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget
```

---

## Quick Start

Follow these five steps to go from zero to a working chatbot.

### Step 1 â€” Install the package

```bash
npm install @hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget
```

### Step 2 â€” Set up Supabase (no code, SQL only)

1. Create a free [Supabase](https://supabase.com) project.
2. Open **SQL Editor** in the dashboard.
3. Print and run the setup SQL from the library:

```js
// Run once in Node to copy the SQL, or read it from the README on npm
import { SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL } from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";
console.log(SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL);
```

4. Paste the output into the Supabase SQL editor and click **Run**.

This creates the `documents` table, HNSW index, `match_documents` search function, and the permissions the browser needs.

### Step 3 â€” Add environment variables

Create a `.env` file in your project (use `VITE_` prefix for Vite, `NEXT_PUBLIC_` for Next.js, etc.):

```env
# Used by the chatbot in the browser
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://yourproject.supabase.co
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-anon-key
VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY=your-gemini-key

# Used by the seed script only â€” never expose in the browser
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=your-service-role-key
```

Find your Supabase keys under **Project Settings â†’ API**.

### Step 4 â€” Seed your content (small Node script)

Create a one-time script with **your** text chunks. The library provides `ingestDocument`; you provide the content.

```js
// scripts/seed-content.mjs
import {
  createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter,
  ingestDocument,
} from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";

const embeddingAdapter = createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter(process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY);

const chunks = [
  {
    content: "We offer web design, SEO, and branding services for small businesses.",
    metadata: { title: "Services", section: "services", url: "/services" },
  },
  {
    content: "Contact us at hello@company.com or call +1 555-0100. Office hours Monâ€“Fri 9amâ€“5pm.",
    metadata: { title: "Contact", section: "contact", url: "/contact" },
  },
];

for (const chunk of chunks) {
  await ingestDocument(
    chunk.content,
    chunk.metadata,
    embeddingAdapter,
    process.env.SUPABASE_URL,
    process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY,
  );
  console.log("Seeded:", chunk.metadata.title);
}

console.log(`Done â€” seeded ${chunks.length} documents.`);
```

Run it once:

```bash
node scripts/seed-content.mjs
```

Use the **same** embedding adapter in your React app as in the seed script so vector dimensions match.

### Step 5 â€” Add the widget to your React app

Create a component (~20 lines) and drop it in your layout:

```tsx
// src/components/MyChatbot.tsx
import {
  ChatbotWidget,
  createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter,
  createGeminiLLMAdapter,
} from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";
import "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget/dist/chatbot.css";

export default function MyChatbot() {
  return (
    <ChatbotWidget
      embeddingAdapter={createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter(import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY)}
      llmAdapter={createGeminiLLMAdapter(import.meta.env.VITE_GEMINI_API_KEY, "gemini-2.5-flash")}
      supabaseUrl={import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_URL}
      supabaseAnonKey={import.meta.env.VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY}
      botName="Support"
      botEyebrow="Chat"
      toggleLabel="Chat with us"
      welcomeMsg="Hi! Ask me anything about our services."
      quickActions={["What services do you offer?", "How can I contact you?"]}
      theme="light"
    />
  );
}
```

```tsx
// src/App.tsx
import MyChatbot from "./components/MyChatbot";

export default function App() {
  return (
    <>
      {/* your app */}
      <MyChatbot />
    </>
  );
}
```

That is the minimum setup. Customize labels, theme, and quick actions via props â€” no pipeline code required.

### What requires code vs what does not

| Task | Code required? |
| --- | --- |
| Install package | No |
| Run Supabase SQL | No |
| Seed documents | Yes â€” small Node script with your content |
| Show chatbot in app | Yes â€” one React component |
| Customize text / theme | Props only |
| Switch AI provider | Change 1â€“2 factory function calls |
| Production API key security | Recommended â€” proxy through your backend (advanced) |

---

## Supported Providers

| Role | Built-in factory | Works via generic factory |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Embedding | Gemini, OpenAI, Cohere | Any HTTP API |
| LLM | Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenAI, Groq, Mistral, Together, Ollama, Anthropic | Any OpenAI-compatible API |

## Database Setup

The library exports ready-to-run SQL:

```tsx
import { SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL } from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";

console.log(SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL);
```

Run the output in your Supabase SQL editor before seeding documents.

**Default schema:** `vector(3072)` for `gemini-embedding-001`. Change the dimension to match your embedding model before creating the table and `match_documents` function.

| Provider & model | Dimensions |
| --- | --- |
| Gemini `gemini-embedding-001` (default) | 3072 |
| Gemini `gemini-embedding-001` (with `outputDimensionality: 768`) | 768 |
| OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | 1536 |
| OpenAI `text-embedding-3-large` | 3072 |
| Cohere `embed-english-v3.0` | 1024 |
| Mistral `mistral-embed` | 1024 |

### Index note (3072 dimensions)

pgvector indexes `vector` columns up to **2000** dimensions. For 3072, `SUPABASE_SETUP_SQL` uses an HNSW index on a `halfvec` cast:

```sql
create index documents_embedding_idx
  on documents using hnsw ((embedding::halfvec(3072)) halfvec_cosine_ops);
```

Storage remains full-precision `vector(3072)`; the index uses half-precision for performance.

### Migrating from vector(768)

If you previously used `text-embedding-004` with `vector(768)`, migrate before re-seeding:

```sql
drop index if exists documents_embedding_idx;
truncate table documents;

alter table documents
  alter column embedding type vector(3072);

create index documents_embedding_idx
  on documents using hnsw ((embedding::halfvec(3072)) halfvec_cosine_ops);

create or replace function match_documents(
  query_embedding  vector(3072),
  match_count      int   default 8,
  match_threshold  float default 0.5
)
returns table (
  id         uuid,
  content    text,
  metadata   jsonb,
  similarity float
)
language sql stable as $$
  select
    id,
    content,
    metadata,
    1 - (embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity
  from documents
  where 1 - (embedding <=> query_embedding) > match_threshold
  order by embedding <=> query_embedding
  limit match_count;
$$;
```

## Document Ingestion

Use `ingestDocument` from a Node.js seed script â€” not from the browser in production:

```js
import {
  createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter,
  ingestDocument,
} from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";

const embeddingAdapter = createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter(process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY);

await ingestDocument(
  "Your document chunk text here.",
  { title: "About", section: "about", url: "/#about" },
  embeddingAdapter,
  process.env.SUPABASE_URL,
  process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY,
);
```

## Usage Examples

### Recommended: Gemini for embedding + answers

```tsx
import {
  ChatbotWidget,
  createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter,
  createGeminiLLMAdapter,
} from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";
import "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget/dist/chatbot.css";

export default function App() {
  return (
    <ChatbotWidget
      embeddingAdapter={createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter("YOUR_GEMINI_KEY")}
      llmAdapter={createGeminiLLMAdapter("YOUR_GEMINI_KEY", "gemini-2.5-flash")}
      supabaseUrl="https://yourproject.supabase.co"
      supabaseAnonKey="YOUR_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"
      botName="Acme Assistant"
      toggleLabel="Chat with us"
      welcomeMsg="Hi! Ask me anything about our products."
    />
  );
}
```

### Gemini embedding + DeepSeek LLM

```tsx
<ChatbotWidget
  embeddingAdapter={createGeminiEmbeddingAdapter("YOUR_GEMINI_KEY")}
  llmAdapter={createDeepSeekLLMAdapter("YOUR_DEEPSEEK_KEY")}
  supabaseUrl="..."
  supabaseAnonKey="..."
/>
```

### OpenAI for both

```tsx
<ChatbotWidget
  embeddingAdapter={createOpenAIEmbeddingAdapter("YOUR_OPENAI_KEY")}
  llmAdapter={createOpenAILLMAdapter("YOUR_OPENAI_KEY", "gpt-4o-mini")}
  supabaseUrl="..."
  supabaseAnonKey="..."
/>
```

### Custom adapters

```tsx
import type { EmbeddingAdapter, LLMAdapter } from "@hasaan_6/rag-chatbot-widget";

const myEmbedding: EmbeddingAdapter = async (text) => {
  return [/* call any API and return number[] */];
};

const myLLM: LLMAdapter = async ({ systemPrompt, question, conversation }) => {
  return { answer: "..." };
};
```

### Custom lead form

```tsx
<ChatbotWidget
  ...
  leadFormConfig={{
    serviceOptions: ["Consulting", "Training", "Custom Build"],
    budgetOptions: ["< $5k", "$5k-$20k", "$20k+"],
    contactTimeOptions: ["This week", "This month", "Flexible"],
    leadKicker: "Tell us about your project",
  }}
  leadEndpoint="/api/leads"
/>
```

## Props Reference

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `embeddingAdapter` | `EmbeddingAdapter` | required | Converts the user question into an embedding vector. |
| `llmAdapter` | `LLMAdapter` | required | Generates the answer from the retrieved context. |
| `supabaseUrl` | `string` | required | Your Supabase project URL. |
| `supabaseAnonKey` | `string` | required | Your Supabase anon/public key. |
| `matchCount` | `number` | `8` | Number of document chunks to retrieve. |
| `matchThreshold` | `number` | `0.5` | Minimum similarity threshold. |
| `conversationWindow` | `number` | `6` | Number of recent conversation turns to include. |
| `botName` | `string` | `AI Assistant` | Heading shown in the chat window. |
| `botEyebrow` | `string` | `Support` | Small eyebrow label above the heading. |
| `toggleLabel` | `string` | `Chat with us` | Visible label on the floating toggle button. |
| `inputPlaceholder` | `string` | `Type your message...` | Placeholder shown in the input box. |
| `welcomeMsg` | `string` | preset | Initial assistant message. |
| `theme` | `"light" \| "dark"` | `light` | Chooses the surface palette. |
| `quickActions` | `string[]` | preset list | Quick action buttons shown above the messages. |
| `fallbackMsg` | `string` | preset | Message shown when the pipeline cannot answer. |
| `leadFormConfig` | `LeadFormConfig` | merged defaults | Controls lead form dropdowns and field visibility. |
| `onLeadSubmit` | `(values: LeadFormValues) => Promise<void>` | undefined | Local submit handler when no lead endpoint is set. |
| `leadEndpoint` | `string` | undefined | Optional POST endpoint for lead submission. |

## Embedding Dimension Reference

| Provider & Model | Dimensions |
| --- | --- |
| Gemini `gemini-embedding-001` (default) | 3072 |
| Gemini `gemini-embedding-001` (with `outputDimensionality: 768`) | 768 |
| OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | 1536 |
| OpenAI `text-embedding-3-large` | 3072 |
| Cohere `embed-english-v3.0` | 1024 |
| Mistral `mistral-embed` | 1024 |

## Theming

The widget uses CSS variables for styling. Override them in your app to match your brand:

```css
:root {
  --chatbot-primary: #6366f1;
  --chatbot-primary-strong: #4f46e5;
  --chatbot-secondary: #111827;
  --chatbot-bg: #ffffff;
  --chatbot-surface: #f9fafb;
  --chatbot-text: #111827;
  --chatbot-muted: rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.62);
  --chatbot-border: rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.12);
  --chatbot-danger: #b91c1c;
  --chatbot-danger-bg: #fef2f2;
  --chatbot-border-radius: 12px;
}
```

Set `theme="dark"` to switch the widget to a dark surface palette.

## Security Note

API keys passed as props are exposed in the browser bundle. Fine for portfolios and demos. For production apps, proxy requests through a server-side route (Next.js route handler, Express endpoint, etc.) and write custom adapters that call your proxy instead of the provider directly.

## Build & Publish

```bash
npm run build
npm publish --access public
```
